mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity cash flows and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the wrong direction.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to separate winning from losing months at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in new home sales and housing starts indicate a collapsing residential demand cycle, which reduces construction spending and consumer wealth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average confirms robust supply-side capacity utilization, supporting corporate earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion phases, or the out-of-sample hit rate drops below 50% across multiple expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising consumer income, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations, or the signal shows zero predictive power in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.0% annually forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, eroding real corporate margins and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0%, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly new home sales confirms broad-based aggregate demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction outweighing demand resilience, compressing profit margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or the regime filter adds no incremental predictive power over a baseline model.
